stat_of_week: 9 SB - Sugar Land Skeeters led the league in steals this week with 9, a category they paired with a 2.11 ERA and 5 QS to run roughshod over the Abbotsford-Colby Twins in an 8-2 blowout
thriller_note: A dead-even tie that saw Buttville dominate ERA and WHIP while General Hospital countered with elite RBI (30) and strikeouts (70), leaving both teams empty-handed in the standings and General Hospital as the league's most unlucky team with an expected wins delta of -0.54 through three weeks.
key_storyline: Hiroshima Radiation exploded for an 8-2 beatdown of the Canberra Wombats, posting league-best marks of 64 hits, a .386 OBP, 9 SB, and 6 QS while Taj Bradley (17 K, 1.59 ERA) and Nolan McLean (15 K, 2.38 ERA) both delivered gems — a statement performance from a team that had been flying under the radar. Meanwhile, the Canberra Wombats got a monster individual week from Gunnar Henderson (5 HR, 8 RBI, .394 OBP) but couldn't get enough support around him, raising serious questions about roster depth beyond their star pieces. The Radiation's week moves them firmly into the upper tier of the power rankings and puts the rest of the league on notice that their pitching-first identity is no fluke.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers won despite ranking near the bottom of the league in HR (5), ERA (5.53), WHIP (1.39), and K (34), surviving almost entirely on Michael King's strong start and a favorable matchup rather than any real roster-wide performance.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs led the entire league in innings pitched (67.0), K (62), QS (5), NSVH (7), and ERA (4.30 — competitive despite the number), yet still lost 5-4 to the Bombers, carrying a luck delta of -0.60 that is the worst in the league this week.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 8-2 win, league-best ERA (2.11), 9 SB, 5 QS, and Matt Olson going 3 HR/8 RBI — the most complete team in the league through three weeks | same
2. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker went 5 HR/6 RBI in a week — five home runs from one player — and Logan Gilbert anchored a rotation that posted 5 QS; still the deepest roster top to bottom | same

3. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood (.636 OBP, 3 HR, 8 R, 8 RBI) and Willson Contreras (3 HR, 11 RBI, .594 OBP) combined for one of the best dual-star performances of the season; 6-3 win cements their top-tier status | up
4. Hiroshima Radiation | Best week of the season: 8 category wins, 6 QS, 75 K, .386 OBP, Bradley and McLean both elite — this rotation is for real and the offense finally showed up | up
5. General Hospital | Tied when their stats said they should have won by a wide margin; 70 K, 30 RBI, and Manny Machado (3 HR, 11 RBI) are legitimately scary, but a 5.48 ERA keeps costing them | same
6. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Lost 5-4 despite leading the league in IP and putting up 5 QS and 7 NSVH — the rotation is elite and this team is severely undervalued by their record | up
7. North Correa | Ohtani (4 HR, .529 OBP) and Chris Sale (22 K) are a devastating one-two punch but the rest of the roster is thin; 11 SB ties the season record and shows their identity | same
8. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Got blown out 8-2 by the Skeeters but still put up 75 K and Yordan Alvarez hit 2 HR; adding Merrill Kelly is a smart, high-floor rotation move | down

9. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6-3 but their pitching ERA (1.65) and WHIP (1.04) were legitimately elite — Taylor Ward, Kris Bubic, and Brandon Woodruff all looked sharp; offense is the persistent weakness | same

10. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison (13 K, 1.74 ERA) and Landen Roupp (10 K, 2.63 ERA) gave them a real rotation backbone; four waiver adds show aggressive management trying to close roster holes | same
11. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6-3 despite zero QS and a 5.04 ERA — Trevor Story's 9 RBI carried them; this team wins ugly and will eventually need pitching to hold | down
12. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz (2 HR, 4 SB) and a 2.25 ERA are the floor here, but a tie when they should have pushed for a win reflects persistent inconsistency | same
13. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson (5 HR, 8 R, 8 RBI) had a truly spectacular week and it still wasn't enough — this roster's depth problem is a structural issue, not a slump | same
14. Benchwarmers | Won on Michael King's back alone; bottom-three in HR, K, ERA, and WHIP — the luck delta of +0.91 is the highest in the league and a regression warning sign is flashing red | same
waiver_highlights: Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A | Added Ildemaro Vargas, Scott Barlow, Dustin May, Masyn Winn | Vargas immediately hit .600 OBP and 4 RBI in his debut week, May adds rotation depth behind an already elite staff, and Winn provides a versatile infield option — this was an excellent four-player haul

Albuquerque Bombers | B | Added Kyle Harrison, Landen Roupp, Braxton Garrett, Josh Jung | Harrison and Roupp both delivered immediately with combined 23 K and sub-2.00 ERAs; this level of active roster management is exactly what a fringe playoff team needs

General Hospital | B | Added Kumar Rocker; dropped Jack Leiter, Janson Junk, Cristian Javier | Clearing three underperformers to make room for Rocker is smart roster hygiene — the rotation ERA of 5.48 this week shows the need for an upgrade was real

Abbotsford-Colby Twins | B | Added Merrill Kelly; dropped Ryne Nelson | A straightforward quality-over-quantity swap — Kelly is a durable, high-floor starter who immediately upgrades an already strong K unit

Riverside Roughnecks | C | Added Victor Vodnik; dropped Mark Vientos | Losing Vientos' positional flexibility and power upside for a bullpen arm on a team with zero QS this week feels backwards — the pitching problems are in the rotation, not the 'pen
